skill.name: xlsx
skill.description: Create, edit, analyze, and validate spreadsheet files with guidance for formulas, formatting, data analysis, visualization, and LibreOffice-based recalculation.
skill.summary: Work with Excel and spreadsheet files while preserving formulas, formatting, and validation workflows.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-severity patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown examples, spreadsheet cell references, legal URLs, and XML namespace text. The confirmed risks are legitimate but elevated: the recalculation helper runs LibreOffice as an external command and writes a persistent macro into the user LibreOffice profile.

medium_findings.title: Persistent LibreOffice Profile Modification
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: recalc.py creates a LibreOffice macro directory in the user profile and writes Module1.xba. This is required for formula recalculation, but it persists outside the workspace and can overwrite an existing standard macro file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly constructs a hidden LibreOffice profile path, creates directories, and writes Module1.xba. The behavior is intentional and useful, but the persistent home-directory write is clearly evidenced.

medium_findings.title: External LibreOffice Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: recalc.py executes soffice and timeout or gtimeout through subprocess.run. Arguments are passed as lists without shell=True, so command injection is not evident, but the skill depends on launching local executables against user workbooks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are explicit and confirmed. Risk is moderated because commands are fixed lists and the workbook path is a separate argument.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Non-Operational References
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: LICENSE.txt contains legal terms URLs, and recalc.py contains an XML namespace URL inside macro content. No evidence found that the skill makes outbound network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in license text and XML metadata, not in networking APIs. No network call is present in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md uses fenced code blocks and inline code for pandas, openpyxl, bash examples, and JSON output examples. These are documentation examples, not executable Ruby or shell backtick calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is Markdown documentation. There is no Ruby code or shell backtick expression in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Spreadsheet Terms Misclassified As Blocker Patterns
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the C2 keyword matches are spreadsheet cell references such as C5, and system reconnaissance matches are formula error names or pandas guidance. Weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary documentation text, not cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is spreadsheet documentation and examples. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior, cryptographic operations, or reconnaissance intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Subprocess Use For Spreadsheet Recalculation
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper uses subprocess.run to launch LibreOffice and timeout wrappers. This is a legitimate implementation pattern for recalculation, but it should be disclosed because it executes local programs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess.run calls are present. The command-injection risk is reduced by list arguments and no shell=True usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Macro File Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper writes Module1.xba under the LibreOffice user profile. This persists outside the project and can affect later LibreOffice sessions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The profile path and file write are explicit. The macro content appears limited to recalculation and save operations.

content.value_statement: Spreadsheet work often fails when formulas, formatting, and recalculation are handled separately. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for creating, editing, analyzing, and validating Excel files.
actual_capabilities: Creates new .xlsx workbooks with formulas, formatting, worksheets, and column sizing.
Reads and analyzes spreadsheet data with pandas workflows for one sheet or all sheets.
Edits existing Excel files while preserving formulas and workbook structure with openpyxl.
Recalculates workbook formulas through the included LibreOffice helper script.
Checks recalculated files for common Excel error values across all worksheets.
Provides financial modeling conventions for number formats, colors, assumptions, and source notes.
limitations: Formula recalculation requires LibreOffice and local command execution.
The helper modifies the LibreOffice user profile by installing a macro file.
The skill provides workflows and examples, not a complete spreadsheet application.
Large workbooks may take longer because validation scans all cells.

use_cases.title: Build Financial Models
use_cases.description: Create workbooks with assumptions, formulas, source notes, and standard financial formatting conventions.

use_cases.title: Clean Operational Data
use_cases.description: Read Excel or CSV data, inspect columns, clean values, and export a structured workbook.

use_cases.title: Validate Template Updates
use_cases.description: Modify existing workbooks while preserving formatting, then recalculate formulas and check for Excel errors.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Simple Workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Excel workbook with one data sheet, clear headers, basic formatting, and formulas for totals.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Spreadsheet Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this spreadsheet, summarize the columns, identify missing values, and create a clean analysis workbook.

prompt_templates.title: Update An Existing Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Modify this workbook using its existing style, keep formulas intact, add the requested data, and validate formula errors.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Financial Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a multi-sheet financial model with assumptions, formulas, source notes, formatting standards, and recalculation validation.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a sales workbook with monthly totals and formatted headers.
output_examples.output: A formatted Excel workbook with a sales data sheet, monthly formulas, total rows, and validated calculated values.

output_examples.input: A user provides an existing budget template and asks for a new forecast tab.
output_examples.output: An updated workbook that preserves the original template style and adds a forecast tab with linked formulas.

output_examples.input: A user asks whether a workbook contains formula errors after edits.
output_examples.output: A concise validation summary listing the workbook status, formula count, and any detected Excel error locations.

best_practices: Use Excel formulas for calculated values so the workbook remains editable and auditable.
Recalculate and validate workbooks after adding or changing formulas.
Preserve existing template styles unless the user requests a redesign.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode calculated values when formulas should remain dynamic.
Do not save a workbook opened with data_only=True if formulas must be preserved.
Do not ignore division by zero, missing references, or sheet name mismatches.

faq.question: What file types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It focuses on Excel workbooks and common tabular files such as .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, and .tsv.

faq.question: Can it preserve formulas in existing workbooks?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow recommends openpyxl for edits that preserve formulas and workbook structure.

faq.question: Can it recalculate formulas?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes recalc.py, which uses LibreOffice to recalculate formulas and report Excel errors.

faq.question: Does recalculation require local software?
faq.answer: Yes. LibreOffice must be installed, and the helper runs local commands to perform recalculation.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for financial models?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes conventions for color coding, number formats, assumptions, source notes, and formula checks.

faq.question: What security warning should users know?
faq.answer: The recalculation helper writes a LibreOffice macro file in the user profile and executes LibreOffice locally.
